skill.name: lark-approval
skill.description: Manage Lark approval instances and approval tasks with the Lark CLI.
skill.summary: Use Lark CLI approval APIs for instances, tasks, and required scopes.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick patterns, a relative path, and a weak cryptography marker, but review found Markdown command examples and documentation text rather than executable code. The skill is medium risk because it depends on an external Lark CLI and documents write-capable approval actions, so users should confirm permissions and intent before mutating approval data.

medium_findings.title: Write-Capable Approval Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Lark approval operations that can approve, reject, transfer, cancel, or copy approval records. This is legitimate functionality, but mistakes can change business workflow state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists mutating approval commands and write scopes. This is a business impact concern, not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are CLI Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick and shell-looking text, but the reviewed lines are Markdown examples for running lark-cli schema and approval commands. No embedded script, command substitution, or automatic execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are inside a fenced Markdown block and inline documentation. They require user-initiated CLI execution and do not execute from the skill file.

low_findings.title: Relative Path Link Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The path traversal finding points to a Markdown link that tells the user to read a shared Lark skill file. It is not used in file I/O code and does not create arbitrary filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative path appears only as a documentation link. There is no code that opens paths based on user input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the Chinese description line. No hash function, encryption routine, or credential handling code is present on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is metadata text describing Lark approval APIs. I found no evidence of weak cryptography in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Run External CLI Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill guides users to run lark-cli commands. This is expected for the skill, but command effects depend on the authenticated Lark account and granted scopes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI dependency is explicit and legitimate. The risk is operational misuse rather than hidden execution.

content.value_statement: Approval teams need accurate commands and scopes when working with Lark approval records. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code inspect schema requirements and run approval instance or task operations through lark-cli.
actual_capabilities: Shows the required lark-cli command pattern for approval API schema lookup.
Lists approval instance actions for get, cancel, and cc operations.
Lists approval task actions for approve, reject, transfer, and query operations.
Maps approval methods to required Lark permission scopes.
Reminds users to inspect schema parameters before sending data or query parameters.
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and authenticated outside the skill.
Does not include full request field schemas inside the skill file.
Does not validate approval business rules before commands are run.
Does not provide rollback guidance for completed approval actions.

use_cases.title: Review an approval instance
use_cases.description: Fetch one approval instance after checking the command schema and required read scope.

use_cases.title: Process pending approval tasks
use_cases.description: Approve, reject, transfer, or query tasks with the documented Lark task methods.

use_cases.title: Prepare integration permissions
use_cases.description: Identify which Lark approval scopes are required before enabling an automation account.

prompt_templates.title: Find the right command
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lark-approval skill to identify the lark-cli command pattern for checking an approval API schema before I run any command.

prompt_templates.title: Check required scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lark-approval skill to tell me which Lark scope is required for querying approval tasks and explain what the scope allows.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a task action
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lark-approval skill to plan the steps for rejecting an approval task. Include the schema check step and the permission scope to verify first.

prompt_templates.title: Design an approval workflow runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lark-approval skill to draft a runbook for approval instance retrieval, task query, task transfer, and required preflight checks.

output_examples.input: Which scope do I need to approve a Lark approval task?
output_examples.output: You need the approval task write scope. Check the task schema first, confirm the target task, then run the approval action with lark-cli.

output_examples.input: How should I retrieve an approval instance?
output_examples.output: Start by inspecting the approval instance get schema. Confirm the instance identifier format, then run the instance retrieval command with read permission.

output_examples.input: Can this skill help me cancel an approval instance?
output_examples.output: Yes. It identifies the cancel operation and its write scope. Confirm business approval before running a command that changes workflow state.

best_practices: Run the schema lookup before every approval command that uses parameters or request data.
Confirm the Lark permission scope and target approval record before mutating workflow state.
Use read-only queries first when investigating approval instances or task queues.
anti_patterns: Do not guess request fields without checking the schema command first.
Do not run write actions from an account with broader permissions than needed.
Do not approve, reject, transfer, or cancel records without an auditable business reason.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users work with Lark approval instances and approval tasks through documented lark-cli command patterns.

faq.question: Does it install or authenticate lark-cli?
faq.answer: No. lark-cli installation and authentication must be handled before using the documented commands.

faq.question: Can it approve or reject tasks?
faq.answer: It documents the task methods and required scopes. The actual action runs through lark-cli under the user's authenticated account.

faq.question: Why should I check the schema first?
faq.answer: The skill states that users must inspect schema output before sending data or parameters to native API commands.

faq.question: Which Lark scopes are listed?
faq.answer: The skill lists approval instance read and write scopes, plus approval task read and write scopes.

faq.question: Is this safe for production workflows?
faq.answer: It can be used safely with least-privilege credentials, schema checks, and human confirmation before write actions.
